# `forwarded_headers`

Injects `X-Forwarded-For`, `X-Forwarded-Proto`, and `X-Forwarded-Host` headers into upstream requests.

## Configuration Notes

When the client IP is from a trusted proxy, existing `X-Forwarded-For` values are preserved and the client IP is appended. Otherwise, the header is overwritten with the client IP to prevent spoofing.

When `use_standard_header` is `true`, also injects the [RFC 7239] `Forwarded` header with `for`, `proto`, and `host` parameters.

## Configuration

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|---------|-------------|
| `trusted_proxies` | string[] | no | CIDR ranges of trusted proxies whose existing X-Forwarded-For values are preserved (appended to). Untrusted sources have the header overwritten. |
| `use_standard_header` | bool | no | When `true`, also inject the standard [RFC 7239] `Forwarded` header in addition to X-Forwarded-* headers. |

## Example

```yaml
filter: forwarded_headers
trusted_proxies: ["10.0.0.0/8"]
use_standard_header: true
```
